metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the DiffInfo→unified-diff transformation in `get_file_diff`: either (a) reconstruct a unified-diff string from DiffInfo's `diff_header` + `content` hunks, or (b) use the `?intraline=false&whitespace=IGNORE_NONE` query params and confirm the response shape, then document the transformer in the plan and assert it in a unit test with a captured DiffInfo fixture.
findings.finding: The plan's `get_file_diff` method is expected to return per-file diff text assembled into `## Auto-merge diff` and passed as `diff_text` through the existing seam (`adapter.code_review_decision(diff_text=…)` → `produce_code_review_verdict`), but the actual Gerrit REST endpoint `GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/files/{file-id}/diff` returns a structured `DiffInfo` JSON entity (fields: `meta_a`, `meta_b`, `intraline_status`, `diff_header`, `content` array of DiffContent hunks) — NOT a unified-diff text string. The existing seam and `assemble_diff_context` in assemble.py (line 437 of gate_dispatch.py) expect unified-diff format text. No DiffInfo→unified-diff transformation step is specified anywhere in the plan.
findings.location: ## What — `gerrit_client.py: get_file_diff (auto-merge base)` + `assemble_merge_change_context` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` + data-flow description 'assembled string → adapter.code_review_decision(diff_text=…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the `get_file_diff` spec: 'The endpoint returns a DiffInfo JSON entity; reconstruct a unified-diff string by concatenating `diff_header` with content hunks rendered as `@@ … @@\n` + context/insertion/deletion lines, OR alternatively use `GET revisions/{rev}/patch?parent=0` (if Gerrit 3.14.1 supports parent=0 for the auto-merge base on a merge commit) to get a full unified patch without per-file fan-out. Verify the chosen approach with a live fixture alongside the files/ fixture in AC#1.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the exact new `_TAG_SUFFIXES` key(s) for the merge-change variants (e.g. `merge-PASS`, `merge-finding`) and specify how the dynamic integrated-commit count `N` is threaded into `_message_tag` (e.g. a new `n_commits` parameter, or a separate `_merge_message_tag(reason, n)` helper), so AC5 is implementable without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The plan says `_TAG_SUFFIXES` 'reuses existing verdict keys, appending (merge-change, N integrated commits)' for the robot comment tag, but the live `_message_tag(reason)` function performs a strict dict-key lookup (`_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]`). The plan never defines new key names (e.g. `merge-PASS`, `merge-finding`) nor specifies how the dynamic commit count `N` reaches the tag-formatting layer. An executor cannot implement AC5 ('robot comment carries the merge-change tag variant with integrated-commit count') without guessing whether to add new `_TAG_SUFFIXES` keys, change `_message_tag`'s signature, or use a separate formatting path.
findings.location: adapter.py — `_TAG_SUFFIXES` / `_message_tag`; AC5 ('Robot comment carries the merge-change tag variant with integrated-commit count')
findings.suggested_fix: Add two entries to `_TAG_SUFFIXES`: `'merge-PASS': 'PASS (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)'` and `'merge-finding': 'BLOCK — finding (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)'`, then introduce a `_merge_message_tag(reason, n_commits)` helper that formats the count into the template. Non-merge call sites continue to use `_message_tag(reason)` unchanged.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `_TAG_SUFFIXES` in `adapter.py` gains a new key (e.g. `'merge-change'`) for the merge-change robot-comment tag, or whether the tag is assembled outside `_message_tag` — and ensure the implementation avoids a `KeyError` on the existing bare `_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]` lookup.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `adapter._TAG_SUFFIXES` 'reuses existing verdict keys, appending (merge-change, N integrated commits)' for the robot comment tag, but the actual `_message_tag` function does a bare `_TAG_SUFFIXES[reason]` dict lookup that raises `KeyError` on any unknown key. The current dict has exactly 6 keys (PASS, finding, gate-disabled, llm-unavailable, scanner, review-error). The plan does not specify whether a new `'merge-change'` key must be added to `_TAG_SUFFIXES`, or whether the suffix string is assembled outside `_message_tag` and injected differently — leaving the implementation contract ambiguous and the KeyError risk unaddressed.
findings.location: src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py lines 46–66 / What section: adapter.py `_TAG_SUFFIXES` description
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `'merge-change'` key to `_TAG_SUFFIXES` (e.g. `'merge-change': 'PASS (merge-change, {n} integrated commits)'` rendered after lookup), or introduce a separate `_merge_message_tag(n_commits, reason)` helper that does not go through the bare dict lookup.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm in the plan (and add a unit test) that non-merge votes continue to emit exactly the existing tag-suffix format, and that the `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` annotation is applied only on the merge code path.
findings.finding: The plan modifies adapter.py's `_TAG_SUFFIXES` (appending '(merge-change, N integrated commits)') but does not explicitly confirm that non-merge vote tag formatting is unchanged, leaving callers that consume or parse the existing tag format without an acknowledgement that their behavior is preserved.
findings.location: adapter.py bullet / AC item 5
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence under the adapter.py bullet: 'Non-merge reviews continue to use the existing tag format unchanged; the merge-change annotation is injected at call-time, not by altering `_TAG_SUFFIXES` globally.' Add a unit test asserting a normal (non-merge) vote still produces the original tag form.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC#1's live fixture to also cover the per-file `/diff` endpoint (no parent param) for a merge commit, confirming it returns the auto-merge base diff — not just `files/` — and add a corresponding citing test in the fixture test suite.
findings.finding: The plan's fixture verification (AC#1) covers only the `files/` endpoint's auto-merge-base default for merge commits, but the per-file `/diff` endpoint (planned as `get_file_diff`, no parent param) is a separate Gerrit REST surface whose merge-commit no-parent behavior is not separately cited or fixture-tested. The assembled context's 'Auto-merge diff' section is built entirely from per-file `get_file_diff` calls — if this endpoint does NOT default to auto-merge base without a parent param, the LLM receives wrong diffs silently.
findings.location: ## What — `gerrit_client.py: get_file_diff (auto-merge base)` + AC#1 (Live 3.14.1 fixture)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC#1: 'Live 3.14.1 fixture proving (a) files/ with no parent returns the auto-merge-base file map AND (b) GET revisions/{rev}/files/{file-id}/diff with no parent returns the diff against the auto-merge base — both committed to tests with Gerrit REST API citations.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC#1 to require a live 3.14.1 fixture for the per-file `GET revisions/{rev}/files/{file-id}/diff` endpoint (no parent param) on a merge commit, verifying it returns the auto-merge-base diff — not just the `files/` list endpoint.
findings.finding: AC#1 (the 'riskiest assumption' fixture gate) is scoped only to the `files/` endpoint's no-parent auto-merge-base behavior, but the per-file `GET revisions/{rev}/files/{file-id}/diff` endpoint's no-parent behavior for merge commits is a SECOND distinct external API assertion that the entire `## Auto-merge diff` section depends on, and it is neither cited in the Gerrit REST docs section within the plan nor included in the AC#1 fixture requirement.
findings.location: AC#1 — 'Live 3.14.1 fixture proving the files/ auto-merge default is committed with the citing test'; ## What — `gerrit_client.py: get_file_diff (auto-merge base)`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact journald grep pattern (event marker string(s)), offset-file path, and container variable for the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` metric in `observability.sh`, mirroring the `VOTER_ERROR` / `VOTER_CONTAINER` / `VOTER_OFFSET_FILE` pattern — and clarify what 'reason-tagged' means in this grep-count context.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the new CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` will be added to `infra/scripts/observability.sh` 'exactly as `voter_errors`', but `observability.sh` currently has no grep pattern for the new journald event names (`merge_change_409_guard`, `mergelist_fetch_error`) and no `put-metric-data` call for `review_bot_merge_change_errors`. The plan does not specify the grep pattern string, offset-file path, or container name for the new metric, leaving the observability implementation under-specified relative to the modelled `voter_errors` block (which has an explicit `VOTER_ERROR` grep pattern, `VOTER_CONTAINER`, and `VOTER_OFFSET_FILE`).
findings.location: infra/scripts/observability.sh / Observability section of the plan

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the grep pattern (or dedicated stderr marker string analogous to `VOTER_ERROR`) that the new `review_bot_merge_change_errors` section in `observability.sh` should search for, so the implementer knows exactly what `voter.py` must emit to stderr and what the shell script must grep.
findings.finding: The plan directs adding the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` metric to `infra/scripts/observability.sh` 'exactly as `voter_errors` is', but the live observability.sh Section 4 greps for the literal string `VOTER_ERROR` from journald — a specific marker the voter writes to stderr. The new merge-path events (`merge_change_review`, `merge_change_409_guard`, `mergelist_fetch_error`) are different log event names and require a distinct grep pattern/section. The plan does not enumerate the grep string or section structure needed in observability.sh, leaving the implementer without a concrete edit target.
findings.location: Observability section / `infra/scripts/observability.sh`

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Inline the latency budget value (e.g., 'total diff fetch must complete within Xs') instead of referencing commit hash `fc2fb799`.
findings.finding: The latency budget is referenced only via a commit hash (`fc2fb799`) without stating the actual budget value. An executor cannot determine whether the sequential REST fan-out is within budget without chasing an external artifact.
findings.location: What section, assemble.py bullet: 'note the latency budget (fc2fb799)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '(fc2fb799)' with the actual numeric budget, e.g., '(total assembled-context fetch ≤ 8 s wall-clock; see fc2fb799 for rationale)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the numeric latency target (p95 wall-clock) for the merge-change review path inline in the plan or AC, and confirm the bounded sequential diff-fetch strategy meets it (e.g. worst-case call count × observed Gerrit p95 latency ≤ budget).
findings.finding: The latency budget for the new sequential REST fan-out path (commit GET + files GET + N×diff GETs + mergelist GET) is referenced only as a commit hash `(fc2fb799)` — no numeric time-bound or done-definition appears in this plan, leaving reviewers unable to verify the new path meets the budget.
findings.location: assemble.py section — 'note the latency budget (fc2fb799)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, replace '(fc2fb799)' with the actual budget figure, e.g. 'the existing p95 budget of Xs — worst-case N_max diff calls × ~Yms Gerrit p95 = Z s, within budget.' If the budget cannot be stated here, add an AC: '[ ] latency smoke: merge-change review completes within Xs p95 on the running Gerrit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the return type and signature of `assemble_merge_change_context` (e.g. whether it returns a `DiffContext` or a raw `str`, and what populates `changed_files`) so the cap-apportionment protocol is co-located with the schema definition and AC3 is unambiguously testable.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `assemble_merge_change_context(merge_files, file_diffs, mergelist)` as a new function with a described cap-apportionment rule ('mergelist section capped first, diff truncated last; DIFF_CHAR_CAP governs the COMBINED string'), but never specifies the function's return type or whether it returns a `DiffContext` (the existing assemble.py dataclass) or a raw string. The cap-rule protocol is stated only in prose and is not co-located with any schema/type definition, so an executor cannot determine how the output plugs into `produce_code_review_verdict` (which calls `assemble_diff_context` → `DiffContext` internally) without guessing.
findings.location: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` — new `assemble_merge_change_context`; plan 'What' section, assemble.py bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `review_bot_merge_change_errors` adds actionable differentiation beyond the existing `voter_errors` alarm (e.g., separate on-call routing for merge-specific failures vs. general voter failures); if not, collapse merge-path error observability into `voter_errors` (already counting VOTER_ERROR markers from all paths) rather than duplicating the full alarm stack.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` (plus a matching terraform alarm and observability.sh grep block) for merge-path REST failures, but the existing `voter_errors` metric already captures ALL fail-closed error events — including merge-path REST failures, since the plan specifies they use the same `_voter_error()` path (coverage-gap tag). This creates a new metric/alarm/offset-file that is a strict subset of `voter_errors`, amounting to config-surface proliferation without demonstrated differentiation.
findings.location: ## What — Observability bullet; AC line 'Metric + alarm applied'
findings.suggested_fix: If the goal is to distinguish merge-path failures from general voter errors in dashboards, add a structured field to the existing VOTER_ERROR JSON line (e.g., `"path": "merge"`) and filter at the dashboard query layer — without adding a separate metric/alarm. If a separate alarm IS justified (e.g., merge-specific paging policy), document the differentiation rationale explicitly and confirm the 'reason-tagged' CloudWatch approach doesn't conflict with the dimensionless requirement documented in monitoring_s4b.tf.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC 5) that explicitly verifies the robot comment text for the clean-merge / empty-auto-merge-diff case states that the conflict delta was empty.
findings.finding: The plan describes a specific robot comment behavior for the empty-auto-merge-diff case ("robot comment states the conflict delta was empty") but no AC covers this variant — AC 5 only checks for the merge-change tag with integrated-commit count, leaving the empty-diff comment text as an uncovered described deliverable.
findings.location: AC 5 / assemble.py section (empty-auto-merge-diff paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 5: 'Robot comment carries the merge-change tag variant with integrated-commit count; for a clean merge (empty auto-merge diff) the comment explicitly states the conflict delta was empty (unit-tested).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a new Terraform file (e.g. `infra/terraform/monitoring_s2_merge.tf`) to the plan's explicit edit targets and to the story's file_impact list so the `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` for `review_bot_merge_change_errors` is not an unenumerated creation.
findings.finding: The new `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` Terraform resource for `review_bot_merge_change_errors` is described in the plan body and cited as a required deliverable in the Observability AC, but no new Terraform file is listed in the epic's `file_impact` list (which enumerates only the 12 already-tracked paths). This is an unenumerated edit target — whoever claims this story will not find the new `.tf` file in the scope list.
findings.location: Observability section / epic file_impact list / `infra/terraform/`
findings.suggested_fix: Add `{"path": "infra/terraform/monitoring_s2_merge.tf", "reason": "S2: aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm for review_bot_merge_change_errors, modelled on monitoring_s4b.tf"}` to the file_impact list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the alarm action (SNS topic ARN / PagerDuty routing key / existing alerting channel) for `review_bot_merge_change_errors` in the Terraform resource definition, consistent with how alarm actions are wired in `monitoring_s4b.tf`.
findings.finding: The alarm action (SNS topic, PagerDuty hook, or other notification target) for the new `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` resource `review_bot_merge_change_errors` is never specified in the plan. An alarm without an action fires silently — operators will never be paged when merge-change errors spike.
findings.location: Observability section / 'aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm terraform resource modelled on monitoring_s4b.tf'
findings.suggested_fix: In the `aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm` block, add `alarm_actions = [var.ops_sns_topic_arn]` (or whichever SNS/PagerDuty ARN the existing monitoring_s4b.tf uses) so the alarm notifies the on-call channel on state transition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback decision: specify what alternative API approach (e.g., `patch?parent=1` with scope restrictions, or a different endpoint) the executor should use if the live fixture disproves the `files/`-without-parent assumption.
findings.finding: The plan frames the `files/` REST behavior as a 'riskiest assumption' to be proven by capturing a live fixture, but provides no fallback design if the assumption is wrong. This is a 'verify whether' placeholder: the executor cannot proceed with the full implementation plan if the fixture reveals a different API behavior.
findings.location: What section, first bullet: 'FIRST (riskiest assumption): capture a live fixture from the running Gerrit 3.14.1 proving `files/` with no `parent` returns the auto-merge-base file map'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a decision branch: 'If fixture disproves the assumption, use [specific alternative endpoint/approach] instead and update the fixture test accordingly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the fake class name reference from `_FakeGerrit` to `FakeGerrit` throughout the plan and any linked disposition comments to match the actual class name in `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py`.
findings.finding: The plan and the round-3 disposition comment (f0f74d65) consistently name the test fake as `_FakeGerrit` (with a leading underscore), but the actual class in tests/unit/test_review_bot.py is `FakeGerrit` (no underscore). Extending `_FakeGerrit` in the new test code would produce a NameError at test-collection time.
findings.location: Tests section / `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py` / round-3 disposition comment citing f0f74d65
findings.suggested_fix: Replace every occurrence of `_FakeGerrit` in the plan text with `FakeGerrit` (matching line 47 of the test file).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each observability AC: e.g., `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --alarm-names review_bot_merge_change_errors` returning the alarm, and `journalctl -u rebar-review-bot | grep merge_change_review` returning at least one line after the smoke test.
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion 'Metric + alarm applied; log events observable in journald' lacks a concrete proving command for both the CloudWatch alarm correctness and journald observability, making it unmeasurable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Metric + alarm applied; log events observable in journald'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the AC to: 'Metric + alarm verified present (`aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --alarm-names review_bot_merge_change_errors` returns the resource); `merge_change_review`, `merge_change_409_guard`, `mergelist_fetch_error` log lines confirmed in journald output after the live smoke test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the merge-review path — e.g., a config toggle (ENABLE_MERGE_CHANGE_REVIEW) that reverts to fail-closed behavior without a redeploy, and document it in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The plan changes merge-commit handling from fail-closed -1 (current 409 behavior) to a full review-and-vote path, but describes no rollback mechanism (feature flag, config toggle, or back-out procedure) if the new code path produces incorrect verdicts or unexpected failures in production.
findings.location: What section — voter.py merge detection paragraph; AC 7 (live smoke)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single boolean config flag (e.g., REVIEW_BOT_MERGE_REVIEW_ENABLED, defaulting to True post-deploy) that, when set to False via environment variable or config file, causes the bot to revert to the fail-closed -1 path for merge commits; document the flag and revert procedure in Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the merge-change review design decisions: auto-merge-base diffing rationale, cap-apportionment ordering (mergelist first, diff last), merge-detection insertion point, and fail-closed contract.
findings.finding: No ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is planned for the new merge-change review pipeline stage, which introduces a novel architectural pattern (auto-merge-base diffing, separate context assembly path, distinct tag variant) that future maintainers will need to understand.
findings.location: ## Scope / assemble.py section / adapter.py section
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/0XXX-merge-change-review-path.md` covering: (1) why files/ with no parent gives auto-merge base, (2) the cap ordering invariant, (3) the fail-closed contract for every merge-path REST failure, (4) why merge detection belongs inside review_and_vote rather than at the call sites.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the new gerrit_client methods (get_commit, get_merge_files, get_file_diff, get_mergelist) inherit or implement retry/backoff logic, and document the retry policy (e.g., 3 attempts with exponential backoff) before the fail-closed -1 path is taken.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no retry or backoff for the four new Gerrit REST calls (get_commit, get_merge_files, get_file_diff, get_mergelist). A transient network blip in the sequential fan-out would immediately trigger a fail-closed -1 vote without any retry attempt, potentially mis-voting on a healthy change.
findings.location: src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py — new methods section; AC: '409-guard unit test: bare /patch on a merge stays fail-closed; every merge-path REST failure votes -1'
findings.suggested_fix: If gerrit_client already has a retry decorator or base-class method, explicitly state that all four new methods use it. If not, add a `@retry(max=3, backoff=exponential)` decorator (or equivalent) to each new method, retrying on transient HTTP 5xx/network errors only, so fail-closed -1 is reserved for persistent failures.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define and enforce an explicit wall-clock timeout for the entire merge-change REST fan-out (e.g., a `asyncio.wait_for` or per-request HTTP timeout), and specify what happens when it is exceeded (fail-closed -1 with the infra tag).
findings.finding: The plan notes 'latency budget (fc2fb799)' for the sequential per-file diff fan-out but does not specify how the budget is enforced — no timeout value or enforcement mechanism is named, leaving the review thread potentially blocked indefinitely on a slow Gerrit.
findings.location: src/rebar/review_bot/assemble.py — 'per-file diffs fetched only until the combined cap is reached — bounds the sequential REST fan-out; note the latency budget (fc2fb799)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or explicitly add) that the bot's IAM execution role policy permits `cloudwatch:PutMetricData` for the `review_bot_merge_change_errors` metric, and include any necessary IAM policy update in the Terraform changeset.
findings.finding: The metric emission mechanism (`infra/scripts/observability.sh` calling CloudWatch PutMetricData) is a shell script invoked at runtime, not a Terraform-managed resource, meaning the IAM permission for `cloudwatch:PutMetricData` on the new metric namespace/dimensions is never audited by the plan. If the bot's execution role lacks this permission, metric emission silently fails (CloudWatch PUT errors are typically swallowed) and the alarm never transitions out of INSUFFICIENT_DATA.
findings.location: Observability section / 'via infra/scripts/observability.sh exactly as voter_errors'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
